description_long: This is a compact, single-story bungalow constructed primarily from gray stone blocks, featuring a symmetrical, rectangular footprint. The front facade is defined by a central wooden door flanked by two large, grid-patterned glass windows with metal grilles. The roof is a stepped, flat-topped design made of stone slabs, giving the structure a robust and utilitarian appearance. The side and rear walls each have a single, centrally placed window, also with a grid pattern, and the lower portion of the walls is textured with cobblestone. The building sits on a small plot bordered by grass, with a black pathway leading to the entrance and small green shrubs on either side of the door. The overall aesthetic is minimalist and functional, with no visible porch or decorative elements.
description: A sturdy, single-story stone bungalow with a flat, stepped roof, central wooden door, and grid-patterned windows, set on a grassy plot with a black pathway.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with a wooden door, two large grid-patterned windows, and small shrubs flanking the pathway.
description_view_1: The left side view displays a single central window and a cobblestone-textured lower wall, with grass bordering the base.
description_view_2: The rear view features two grid-patterned windows and a cobblestone-textured lower wall, with no entrance or decorative elements.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, with a single central window and cobblestone-textured lower wall, bordered by grass.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with single central side and rear windows
